Total

2,504,000

100.0%

This can be easier to visualize in a pie chart:

Again, the NEV source for both the above spreadsheet and pie chart is the September CPCA NEV report, where we see only BYD has higher numbers than Geely:

The ICE numbers for both the spreadsheet and pie chart are obtained by taking the overall wholesale numbers and subtracting the NEV wholesale numbers. Here is the source for the overall wholesale numbers from the September CPCA express report:

*Some of the joint ventures ("JVs") are too small on the NEV side to have their own NEV spreadsheet entries and their own NEV pie slices. Still, we subtract their NEV units from their overall units to get their ICE numbers. FAW-VW had 5,746 monthly NEV wholesale units, SAIC-VW had 13,784 and GAC Toyota had 356. We subtract these from their monthly overall wholesale units of 148,285, 94,000 and 72,278, respectively, to get their monthly ICE wholesale units of 142,539, 80,216 and 71,922, respectively.

Most of the above NEVs are BEVs. Per the September CPCA analysis report, the NEV portion of the overall wholesale market above was 1,231 thousand vehicles, composed of 723 thousand BEVs, 394 thousand PHEVs and 114 thousand extended range electric vehicles ("EREVs"). Seeing as China is a prodigious part of the world's BEV market, Geely compares well with other global companies when we look at global BEV sales:

*I believe the graphic above excludes SAIC-GM-Wuling because their BEVs are closer in size to golf carts than typical cars. Chery isn't in the top 12 above because many of their NEVs are PHEVs. Li Auto doesn't make the top 12 above because most of their NEVs are EREVs.

Geely is on track to continue their growth of NEV units seeing as they are an innovator with electrification and digitalization and these are important considerations for auto buyers in China. STMicroelectronics recently discussed their collaboration with Geely, and this gets me excited about Geely's future NEV sales (emphasis added):

Geely Auto, is a shining example of automotive innovation in China, making rapid progress in car electrification and digitalization, while expanding its presence in the global market. This long-term SiC supply agreement and the joint lab establishment mark a significant step forward in our long-established cooperation," said Henry Cao, Executive Vice President of Sales & Marketing, China Region, STMicroelectronics. "China is the biggest NEV market worldwide and a leading innovator."

Valuation

One of the most important considerations regarding the valuation range is the way the gross margin can change as NEVs become a higher percentage of overall sales. We know the economics are tough for BEVs at this time, but there is evidence Geely has figured out how to make the numbers work with PHEVs. This is because PHEV units increased 489% from the first half of 2023 to 130,238 units in the first half of 2024, but overall gross margins weren't hurt. The 2024 interim report came out on August 21st, and it says the following about gross margins (emphasis added):

Although price competition in the PRC automobile market has further intensified and the proportion of NEVs in sales volume of the Group has increased, the gross profit margin of the Group in the first half of 2024 increased by 0.7 percentage point YoY to 15.1%, as a result of an optimized product mix and cost control.

Geely GEO Sheng Yue Gui said BYD only makes money on PHEVs in Geely's 4Q23 call. I'm assuming this is the case for Geely themselves, they make money on the PHEV side of the NEV business but the BEV side of the NEV landscape will require patience as we're not yet in a world where customers are willing to pay a high price for the software side of the vehicle (emphasis added):

In China's carmaking world, there was a unique phenomenon. Chinese carmakers are making money. As long as you have your own powertrain or engine production, you'd be able to make money. But if you are a pure NEV maker, you're not making money. BYD is excellent in pure EV, but they're only making money for their hybrid models, whereas Toyota, they're making money.

A business is worth the amount of cash which can be pulled out of it from now until judgment day. Some companies are better than others with respect to the extent accrual profits can help us with this approximation. It is important for us to concentrate on ongoing profitability as opposed to one-off gains.

Per the 1H24 interim results, the 1H24 profit to shareholders figure of RMB 10,598 million includes some extraordinary considerations which are not representative of regular earning power (emphasis added):

The business model of Lynk&Co JV in Europe is still being actively optimized. Although Lynk&Co JV still recorded a net loss of RMB 0.25 billion during the period, its electrification transformation strategy in the PRC market has achieved phased results, resulting in an increase in the total sales volume and the proportion of NEV sales volume of the Lynk&Co JV, and a YoY decrease in its net loss by 63%. In addition, the Group contributed assets to Horse Powertrain Limited ("Horse Powertrain"), a joint venture company, during the period, and made a valuation adjustment for the partial disposal of Horse Powertrain, recording a one-off gain on deemed disposal of subsidiaries and an impairment loss on assets classified as held for sale, amounting to RMB 7.47 billion. As a result of the above, the Group's profit attributable to equity holders of the Company in the first half of 2024 was RMB 10.60 billion, representing a YoY increase of 575%. Diluted earnings per share was RMB1.04. Excluding the aforesaid one-off gain on deemed disposal of subsidiaries and the impairment loss on assets classified as held for sale, as well as the total impairment loss on non-financial assets, the profit attributable to equity holders of the Company for the period was RMB 3.37 billion, representing a YoY increase of 114%.

Looking at the 1H24 interim results and the 2023 annual results, we have trailing twelve month ("TTM") numbers. Regarding 1H24 profit to shareholders, I use the adjusted RMB 3.37 billion figure and not the one-off gain figure of RMB 10.6 billion. Adjusted TTM profit to shareholders is RMB 7,107 million or RMB 3,370 million + RMB 5,308 million - RMB 1,571 million. TTM revenue is RMB 213,327 million or RMB 107,305 million + RMB 179,204 million - RMB 73,182 million. Converting to dollars, this equates to adjusted profit to shareholders of $1 billion and revenue of $30 billion.

Geely's current sales volume of NEVs relative to 3 years ago is fantastic, and they have many advantages over other automakers, but they're still in the historically tough automobile business and I don't think the multiple on adjusted profit should be more than 15 or 16x, implying a valuation range of $15 to $16 billion.

GELYF equates to ordinary shares, and Citigroup says one GELYY share equals 20 ordinary shares. There were 10,063,382,383 shares as of June 30 and the market cap is about $15.3 billion based on the October 17 GELYF share price of $1.52. Their market cap is within a reasonable valuation range and I think the stock is a hold.

Disclaimer: Any material in this article should not be relied on as a formal investment recommendation. Never buy a stock without doing your own thorough research.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Nvidia Bounces Toward Our Target Of $165 (Technical Analysis)
data.description:
As indicated in our last article, Nvidia (NASDAQ:NVDA) had a short-term Sell signal and a longer-term Buy signal. That short-term signal provided investors with the opportunity to buy a long-term investment on price weakness. NVDA had a perfect score of 100. As you can see on our Model Portfolio Watchlist below, NVDA is still at the top of our list, with the highest Stocks In Demand or SID score in the SID column, but has dropped from 100 to 90. Our computer is calculating a 26% gain in 12 months and our target price is ~$165.

Here is our 2025 Model Portfolio Watchlist with NVDA at the top. Notice on our 20-day bar chart how red bars of Supply improved to black bars of Demand, but no green bars indicating buyers chasing price. Portfolio Managers will wait for the next pullback in price to buy, if they are not already maxed out on how much of NVDA they are allowed to own.

As you know from our previous articles, our SID system uses both fundamentals and technicals to come up with our SID score. Here we have to do our due diligence on the SID computer system. We do that by first analyzing the charts for the technicals. Then we go to the SA analyst ratings and Quant ratings for the fundamentals.

Here is the longer term, more reliable signals on the weekly chart, and you can see all the technical buy signals. However, our SID Buy signal at the top of the chart is unlike the other purely technical signals on the chart because it uses both fundamentals and technicals.

Note on the above chart that between the vertical red line technical Sell signal and the blue line technical Buy signal, the sellers failed to trigger a Sell signal at the top of our chart showing our SID signal. This confirmed that the pullback was a buy on weakness opportunity. Now with Buy signals for the SID, ADX, MACD, Full Sto and Performance we think price is targeting ~$165.

Now let's go to the SA signals. SA analysts have a consensus Buy rating, while the Wall St. analysts have a strong Buy. The SA Quant rating is a Hold, so we feel comfortable with our SID rating that just improved from a Hold to a Buy rating.

The SA Quant rating shows strong grades for Growth, Profitability, Momentum and Revisions, but a weak grade for Valuation. It is difficult to accept a target of $165 when the forward PE is 48. The market obviously is expecting higher targets as future earnings are reported. If that fails to happen, then the price will drop.

The consensus Wall St. analyst target is $149, but the highest target is $203. In October, there are a large number of analysts who have switched from a Buy to a Hold rating. That would reflect the weak Valuation grade given by the SA Quant system.

NVDA needs to come in with an excellent earnings report. The CEO thinks the growth is good, but that may not live up to the high expectations of the market. Any pullback after earnings will probably trigger our buy on weakness signals, which we show in the Tmg and Cfrm columns of our daily report, shown above. We will keep you posted. Or just message us if you want an update on our signals, and we will post them as part of this article.

data.title: Warner Bros. Discovery: Facing Two-Front Battle In Key Area
data.description:
Stability has never really been a strong suit when it comes to Warner Bros. (NASDAQ:WBD) and its various parent companies over the past few years. Its final Time Warner years were a mess, it’s short-lived marriage with AT&T was surprisingly somehow not much better, and its current Warner Bros. Discovery merger has been shaky from the jump.

And now we’ve reached a point that investors are looking for anything to cling to as a reminder of days gone by – before COVID, streaming and a number of other factors. Unfortunately for those investors, that hope hasn’t come easy. In the past few months, WBD was handed a two-front loss in a key area that is currently fueling a lot of its rivals.

However, could things be starting to turn around?

First as always, some background.

David Zaslav’s decisions as WBD topper have been in many ways as expected.

A cost-cutter to the core, Zaslav and his unique management style has been slashing budgets, content and sadly jobs at every turn.

However, the cuts went deeper in some areas than first assumed… now instead of just canceling underperforming shows, Zaslav’s team cancelled shows that hadn’t even aired. Full seasons of series and mostly completed films were shelved simply for the write-off. It is the type of thing that could become a running joke if it wasn’t so depressing.

However, Zaslav’s frugalness eventually went a little too far though, as over the summer WBD tried to play hardball with the NBA over its upcoming license renewal.

It didn't go well.

I’m not going to go too deep here, but the short version is WBD had a “match” clause in its contract that allowed it to match any offer from rivals looking to take over its NBA package. Zaslav’s team waited until the last minute to enact that clause, only to be rebuffed when the NBA determined what WBD was offering wasn’t a complete match and it wasn’t legally obligated to accept.

The two sides appear to be heading to court, but the presumed result is likely WBD losing the NBA after this season and those games going to Amazon.

Around the same time, Venu, WBD’s joint-venture with Disney and FOX, was delayed indefinitely by a lawsuit brought by streaming sports network Fubo. The suit alleged anti-competitive practices by the trio, and a judge granted an injunction against its launch.

Again, I’m not going to go deep here, but I had to set the stage.

You can see why investors may be spooked.

And with the NBA about to return next week, you can also see why things may again get worse for WBD. Yes, they have the NBA this season, but assuming they lose their legal challenge, this year will also mark the end of that longtime partnership and its signature Inside The NBA series. The studio show featuring Ernie Johnson, Kenny Smith, Charles Barkley and Shaquille O’Neal has been one of the most entertaining and engaging sports broadcasts on TV. The Emmy-winning franchise is as much a part of the NBA season as the teams and players.

Or it was.

Investors are going to be reminded of this every week and that’s not something that will work to Zaslav’s benefit… especially if the ratings hold.

Yet, there has been some good news for WBD investors, and it has come from unexpected places.

WBD’s recent carriage renewals with Charter yielded a truly surprisingly outcome. Despite soon to be losing the NBA, Charter will continue to pay the same costs to WBD to access its programming for its subscribers, and higher costs for additional WBD channels.

Yes, in a world where carriage feuds over pricing are leading to blackouts, Charter didn’t go in for the kill. Instead, Charter worked out a deal where WBD’s streaming service Max (and its HBO offerings) would now be offered to its customers at no charge.

It is a win for both sides, but especially for Zaslav and WBD, as it was a completely unexpected development and there is hope they can use it as a framework for future deals.

Chief among those deals is one with Comcast, which is expected to be contentious.

Comcast is not only also getting a package of NBA games, but remember they were excluded from the Venu deal. That doesn’t exactly present the best look going into these negotiations.

So what is WBD looking to use as proof-points here and in other cases to show they don’t need the NBA – well it won’t be Venu, which even if the injunction gets lifted and the product launches, nobody is quite sure how it will be received.

Instead, WBD will be looking to showcase that while the NBA was an important piece of its sports portfolio, it was just that… a piece.

WBD also has deals with the NHL, NASCAR, MLB (including playoffs) and various soccer games. On top of that, it has its longer-term NCAA Final Four deal in place with CBS as a partner. Suffice it so say, WBD viewers will still get plenty of competitive action.

There’s also a rising player in the space that has been homegrown WBD from day one – AEW.

If you don’t know what AEW is, you likely aren’t alone, but you’ll soon understand its value. AEW stands for “All Elite Wrestling” and it has emerged in recent years as a growing rival to the WWE machine (now owned as part of TKO along with UFC).

Backed by the Kahn family (of Jacksonville Jaguar fame) the professional wrestling organization has slowly grown its audience and prominence in the space. Fueled by a collection of talent (including a number of WWE defectors) that has appealed to audiences, the brand brings back memories of when Turner Networks had World Championship Wrestling (WCW).

Wrestling roots run deep in the South and had long been at the core of TNT as a network, so you can see why AEW took hold and has held its own. While not being as popular as it used to be, it is still an important player and one that WBD knows its needs.

Recently, the pair renewed its partnership and, as a result, will now have its PPV’s be streamed for free on Max – akin to what WWE does with Peacock for its premium live events. That’s not a small thing either and will help grow the brand.

Remember, it also wasn’t a guarantee AEW and WBD would stay together – with the WWE to Netflix shift happening in 2025, the board was cleared for a big move if AEW wanted to make one. However, this was a deal it liked and honestly was probably the one it needed.

The same can be said for its “Unrivaled” basketball deal that is just announced will launch in 2025. Many companies as of late have leaned into the red-hot WNBA brand and “Unrivaled” will represent the next evolution of that escalation.

WBD’s TNT will air 45 games over a three-month period that will feature six teams of five players in a 3-on-3 setup. Created by WNBA icons Breanna Stewart and Napheesa Collier, as well as backed by past female sports legends like Alex Morgan and Megan Rapinoe, there’s a lot to like about this type of league.

For WBD, though, one of the big selling points is likely the timing – the league will run pre-WNBA season which is that January-March timeframe… in other words when the NBA would normally be heating up. This will give TNT the ability to plug the hole and to do so with a brand that presents them with more of a financial stake.

Now imagine if they are able to slide the current “Inside The NBA” team to “Unrivaled.” It’s one thing to have the backing they have and a number of WNBA talent committed to play, but having that core four be part of the studio team only adds to its allure.

Bankrolling sports licensing rights is hard because it's costly and with all the debt WBD had coming out of its prior deal, Zaslav has to be strategic in what he thought had the most value. The issue for investors is more that while these were all business decisions, there was still emotion tied to each one.

It was a cat-and-mouse game, and the cat didn’t always win. WBD tried to get too clever with the NBA and WBD tried to one-up Comcast with Venu and those miscues are going to haunt them and their shareholders for a while.

Although, you have to give Zaslav and his team credit for what they did accomplish, as for now it seemingly has done the job. But is it a long-term fix? I’m not convinced it is, but they’ve bought themselves enough time to make the case.
